title: [Debug] Add expression support to HGLDD emission
body:
Extend `EmitHGLDD` to properly represent combinational expressions in the emitted HGLDD debug info file. This is a first prototype to experiment with recreating higher-level source language values from the low-level signals that survived all the way through Verilog emission.

Instead of just checking if `dbg.variable` ops directly point at something in the MLIR that has a public Verilog name, the emitter now looks through expressions, `dbg.struct`s, and `dbg.array`s, and builds up the equivalent expression in HGLDD. As a result, the emitted debug info file now contains enough for a debugger to reconstruct many of the interesting source language values from the values that are actually present in the output Verilog.

At a later point we might want to move some of the expression graph construction into the `DebugInfo` analysis. But at the moment it isn't totally clear to me whether looking through the IR proper is an acceptable approach, or whether we'd want to create a "shadow expression graph" in the analysis that is decoupled from the IR.
